title: Executive Order Modifying Delegations of Presidential Authority Under the Defense Production Act
summary: This executive order amends Executive Order 13603 from March 16, 2012, which established the National Defense Resources Preparedness framework. The order adjusts and modifies certain delegations of presidential authority that were previously granted under the Defense Production Act of 1950. The Defense Production Act grants the President broad powers to direct industrial resources and economic activity during periods of national emergency or defense needs. This order appears to reassign, clarify, or expand the delegation of these powers among various executive branch agencies and officials, though the specific modifications are not detailed in the provided excerpt.

title: Executive Order on Verification Requirements for Products Advertised as Made in America
summary: This executive order establishes policy regarding the advertising and labeling of products claimed to be made in the United States. The order asserts that consumers have a right to clear, accurate, and substantiated information about whether products marketed as made in America are actually manufactured in the United States. The order aims to establish protections for American consumers against potentially misleading claims in product advertising related to domestic origin. The order delegates authority to relevant agencies to develop and implement standards for verifying and substantiating such claims.

title: Executive Order on Reducing Regulatory Requirements for Residential Housing Construction and Renovation
summary: This executive order directs the federal government to identify and reduce regulatory requirements that the order characterizes as unnecessary barriers to the construction and renovation of residential housing. The order states that its purpose is to support homeownership by enabling a more dynamic housing market with increased inventory of newly built and renovated homes. The order authorizes the President to direct relevant federal agencies to review existing regulations affecting residential construction and renovation activities and to take action to streamline or modify such regulations.

title: Executive Order on Mortgage Credit Access and Lending Standards
summary: This executive order establishes directives related to mortgage lending practices and access to home financing. The order articulates a policy objective that Americans seeking to purchase homes should have access to mortgage credit from reliable lenders, with interest rates determined by individual creditworthiness assessments. The order appears to address mortgage lending standards and credit availability in the residential housing market, though the full text of the directives and implementing provisions is not provided in the excerpt.

title: Presidential Proclamation on Women's History Month 2026
summary: A presidential proclamation issued in March 2026 recognizing and commemorating the contributions of American women throughout the nation's history. The proclamation acknowledges women's roles and achievements across various sectors including family, culture, military service, industry, and national development over the course of approximately 250 years of American history.

title: Presidential Nominations and Withdrawal of Nominations Sent to Senate
summary: The President sent to the Senate a nomination of Sarah B. Rogers of New York for the position of Chief Executive Officer of the United States Agency for Global Media. Additionally, the President withdrew the nomination of William Hewes III of Mississippi for the position of Commissioner of the Consumer Product Safety Commission, a position with a seven-year term beginning October 27, 2024.

title: Presidential Memorandum Regarding Compensation Practices at the Tennessee Valley Authority
summary: This presidential memorandum directs the Board of Directors of the Tennessee Valley Authority to review and modify compensation practices within the organization. The memorandum is issued under presidential authority granted by the Constitution and the Tennessee Valley Authority Act of 1933. The document addresses compensation structures and practices at the federal agency responsible for regional development, power generation, and environmental stewardship in the Tennessee Valley region.

title: Presidential Nominations for Secretary of Homeland Security and United States Marshals
summary: Nomination of Markwayne Mullin of Oklahoma for the position of Secretary of Homeland Security. Additionally, nominations of James Arnott of Missouri for United States Marshal for the Western District of Missouri for a four-year term and Jack Chambers of West Virginia for United States Marshal for the Southern District of West Virginia for a four-year term. These nominations have been transmitted to the United States Senate for consideration and confirmation.

title: Proclamation Designating U.S. Hostage and Wrongful Detainee Day, 2026
summary: A presidential proclamation establishing U.S. Hostage and Wrongful Detainee Day in 2026. The proclamation expresses support for American citizens held captive or wrongfully detained abroad and reaffirms the government's commitment to addressing cases of Americans in such situations. The proclamation acknowledges the circumstances faced by detained individuals and commits to continued engagement regarding their status.
